images.desc: LEADS Higher Secondary School, Langkerding, is honoured to receive a letter of appreciation from Shri Vijay Kumar Mantri, IAS, Commissioner & Secretary, Education Department, Government of Meghalaya, congratulating the school for achieving a remarkable 100% pass result in the SSLC Examinations. The recognition highlights the dedication of our students, teachers and leadership in fostering academic excellence and a strong learning environment. This achievement marks a proud milestone in our journey of excellence.

LEADS secured first place at the Green-Preneurship 2026 competition, held at BDW International School, the event was sponsored by Meghalaya Prime Startup, competing against 10 participants from premier institutes across Shillong. This achievement has significantly increased LEADS’ visibility in schools across the city.

Check out these stunning masterpieces by our Class IX students, created during their Creative Expressions co-curricular sessions. From bold strokes to intricate details, these young artists are turning blank canvases into vibrant expressions of their unique vision. 🎨🖌️

Miss Narolla Sangma poses with one of her signature pieces, showcasing the talent behind our Creative Expressions program. Her mentorship continues to inspire our Class IX students to explore their own artistic boundaries and create beauty. 🎨🖌️
The future looks bright! Introducing our newly felicitated school prefects for 2026. Empowered to lead, inspired to serve.

Newly appointed Captains and Assistant Captains proudly stand alongside the Principal of LEAD Higher Secondary School, marking the beginning of a new chapter of leadership, responsibility and inspiration.
Students sharing smiles, stories and lunch under the greenery of their school eco-park.
The Parent–Teacher Meeting for the First Terminal 2026 was successfully held, sharing key instructions for students’ welfare.

In a quiet room heavy with silence, the boundary between human and insect blurs—echoing the haunting solitude of Gregor Samsa. This evocative illustration by Lingthoinganbi Maibam captures the fragile stillness and alienation at the heart of The Metamorphosis by Franz Kafka, where transformation is not merely physical, but deeply emotional and existential.
